title: Art Thieves Steal Famous Paintings in Italy
summary: In Italy, near the city of Parma, a museum was robbed by four masked men. They stole three valuable paintings by famous artists: Renoir, Cézanne, and Matisse. The thieves broke into the museum, took the paintings in just three minutes, and escaped by climbing over a fence. An alarm went off, which stopped them from stealing more art. The paintings are worth millions of euros, with one by Renoir alone worth €6 million. Police are now investigating the theft. This museum is called the Magnani Rocca Foundation, and it was set up after an art collector named Luigi Magnani died. Art thefts like this have happened before, such as at the Louvre in Paris last year.

questions.question: How many masked men stole the paintings?
How long did the thieves take to steal the paintings?
What stopped the thieves from stealing more paintings?
Who set up the Magnani Rocca Foundation?

background_read: Art theft is when people steal valuable paintings or other artworks from museums or galleries. This can happen quickly, and thieves often use masks to hide their faces. Museums have alarms to protect their art. Famous artists like Renoir, Cézanne, and Matisse created beautiful paintings that are worth a lot of money. Italy has many museums with important art, and police work hard to solve thefts and keep art safe for everyone to enjoy.
Article_Structure: WHO: Four masked thieves and police investigators
WHAT: Stole three valuable paintings by Renoir, Cézanne, and Matisse from a museum
WHERE: Near Parma, Italy, at the Magnani Rocca Foundation villa
WHY: To steal art worth millions of euros, but an alarm stopped them from taking more

perspectives.description: The theft shows that museums need strong alarms and protection to stop thieves.
